description: Neutral Opening Book for Engine Matches - Let chess-runner apply an optional neutral opening line to both engines before search begins, preserving fairness and reducing time spent on basic opening theory.

userStories.title: Define efficient opening book format
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an efficient opening-book source format so that chess-runner can load neutral opening lines from repository data or local files with low implementation overhead.
acceptanceCriteria: Use a plain-text opening-book format with one opening line per file line
Each opening-book line stores UCI moves separated by spaces from the initial position
Empty lines are ignored during loading
The format can be loaded from a repository file or a local external file
CLI help or adjacent documentation states which initial format is supported
`cargo build --workspace` passes
`cargo test --workspace -- --skip gen_files::magics::name` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Opening book format defined: plain-text, one UCI move sequence per line, comments (#) and empty lines ignored. Module at chess-runner/src/opening_book.rs, sample file at chess-runner/openings.txt.

userStories.title: Add explicit opening-book flag to chess-runner match
userStories.description: As a developer, I want opening-book support to be enabled only through an explicit match flag so that existing match behavior remains unchanged unless I opt in.
acceptanceCriteria: `chess-runner match` exposes an explicit flag to provide an opening-book source
`chess-runner match` exposes `--opening-book-seed <u64>` for reproducible opening-line selection
`chess-runner match` exposes `--opening-book-max-ply <n>` to limit how many opening-book plies may be applied
Without the opening-book flag, match behavior remains unchanged
`chess-runner match --help` documents how to enable the opening book and the related seed and max-ply options
`cargo build --workspace` passes
`cargo test --workspace -- --skip gen_files::magics::name` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added --opening-book, --opening-book-seed, and --opening-book-max-ply flags to MatchArgs in chess-runner/src/main.rs. Also updated PgnMatchArgs delegate to populate the new fields with defaults.

userStories.title: Load and validate opening-book lines
userStories.description: As a developer, I want chess-runner to load opening-book lines and reject malformed input early so that matches fail fast on invalid book data.
acceptanceCriteria: `chess-runner` loads the opening-book source before match execution starts
Each loaded line is validated as a legal move sequence from the initial position
Invalid lines produce a descriptive error and non-zero exit status
If the validated book is empty, execution fails with a clear message
`cargo build --workspace` passes
`cargo test --workspace -- --skip gen_files::magics::name` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added validate_opening_book() to chess-runner/src/opening_book.rs — replays each line from Board::default() using ChessMove::from_uci(), collects all errors and returns them together, then fails if the validated set is empty. In run_match(), loads and validates the book early (before engine name queries) so the process exits fast on bad data.

userStories.title: Select opening-book lines uniformly with seed support
userStories.description: As a developer, I want chess-runner to choose uniformly among valid opening lines so that matches do not always repeat the same theory branch and selection remains reproducible.
acceptanceCriteria: When multiple valid lines exist, selection is uniformly random
Selection uses the `--opening-book-seed` value when provided
The same seed and same input choose the same line
Without an explicit seed, selection still succeeds using a non-deterministic seed source
`cargo build --workspace` passes
`cargo test --workspace -- --skip gen_files::magics::name` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added select_opening_line(lines, seed) to chess-runner/src/opening_book.rs using SmallRng::seed_from_u64 + SliceRandom::choose. In run_match(), load/validate/select happen together early; selected line stored as _selected_opening for US-005 to consume.

userStories.title: Apply opening-book moves before engine search
userStories.description: As a developer, I want chess-runner to apply the same opening line before search begins so that both engines start from an identical neutral position.
acceptanceCriteria: Before the first computed engine move, `chess-runner` applies the selected opening line to the game state
Both engines receive the same resulting position after the opening line is applied
Opening-book moves do not consume engine clock time
The effective starting game state matches the applied move sequence
`cargo build --workspace` passes
`cargo test --workspace -- --skip gen_files::magics::name` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added opening_line_to_fen(line) to chess-runner/src/opening_book.rs — replays moves from Board::default() and formats the resulting Board via Display (FEN). In run_match(), opening_start_fen is computed from the selected opening and passed as the effective start_fen to play_games(), overriding args.start_fen. Clock only starts when engines search, so opening moves cost no clock time.

userStories.title: Add configurable ply limit for opening-book application
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the opening book to stop after a configurable early-game ply limit so that engines still perform their own middle-game search.
acceptanceCriteria: `chess-runner match` uses the `--opening-book-max-ply` value to limit how many opening-book plies may be applied
`chess-runner` never applies opening-book moves beyond that ply limit
After the limit is reached, the game continues normally with engine search
Without opening-book configuration, existing behavior remains unchanged
`cargo build --workspace` passes
`cargo test --workspace -- --skip gen_files::magics::name` passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Updated opening_line_to_fen(line, max_ply) to accept a max_ply parameter and truncate the replay with .take(max_ply). In run_match(), passes args.opening_book_max_ply (default 20) to the function. Added tests for zero-ply (returns initial position FEN) and truncation.

userStories.title: Log chosen opening-book line in debug output
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the selected opening-book line to appear in debug output so that I can audit which neutral opening was applied without changing result schemas.
acceptanceCriteria: When the opening book is enabled, `chess-runner` logs which opening line was chosen in debug output
The debug log contains enough information to identify the applied sequence or its identifier
Without the opening-book flag, this debug log does not appear
`cargo build --workspace` passes
`cargo test --workspace -- --skip gen_files::magics::name` passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pass opening-book configuration through benchmark script
userStories.description: As a developer, I want benchmark tooling to optionally pass opening-book configuration into chess-runner so that neutral openings can be reused consistently in automated evaluations.
acceptanceCriteria: `scripts/benchmark-version.sh` or equivalent benchmark flow can pass opening-book configuration to `chess-runner`
When opening-book configuration is omitted, benchmark behavior remains unchanged
When enabled, the benchmark applies the same opening-book mechanism neutrally to both engines
`bash -n scripts/benchmark-version.sh` passes
`cargo build --workspace` passes
`cargo test --workspace -- --skip gen_files::magics::name` passes
Typecheck passes
